author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

Shipped v2.5: `Status::Blocked` + `block_reason: Option<String>` field +
`Op::SetBlockReason` + `jjf block/unblock` verbs + `jjf ready
--include-blocked` flag. Scalar LWW resolver handled the new reason
field cleanly with **zero new resolver machinery** — the existing
`(jjf_at, commit, trailer_index)` total order in `merge_ops::reduce_to_merged`
already covers any scalar; one line added to the per-op `match` (mirroring
`SetSlug`'s shape) was the entire reducer change. The 34 resolver unit
tests plus the new tests went green on the first try.

Lifecycle decisions:
- Variant order: `Open | Blocked | InProgress | Closed` (natural progression).
- `is_active` (dep-blocking) treats Blocked as ACTIVE — a blocked dep
  still blocks dependents (parked, not closed).
- Slug uniqueness widened to Open/Blocked/InProgress.
- `claim` rejects Blocked issues (forces explicit `unblock` to preserve
  audit trail).
- Multi-line reasons rejected at storage boundary (would corrupt the
  `Jjf-Reason:` trailer).

Test count: 366 → 387 (+21 new). 6/6 smoke-test steps pass from the
source repo (block / ready excludes / ready --include-blocked includes /
show renders reason / unblock / restore).

## Recommendation

Merge as-is. Implementation matches the design doc verdict (§6 of
`docs/agent-await-gates-design.md`) exactly. v2.5 spec bump landed in
both `docs/storage-format.md` (schema + op vocab + changelog) and
`docs/cli-json.md` (envelopes + status enum).

## Confidence

High. The scalar-LWW pattern is well-trodden (5th time we've extended
it: title, body, assignee, type, slug — now reason). Same-shape spec
bump as v2.3's InProgress, well-understood mechanics throughout.

## Open follow-ups

- None blocking. Possible future: a `--unblock-by <id>` filter on
  `jjf ready` ("what would become workable if X unblocks") — separate
  agent-ergonomics ticket if it ever earns priority.
